subject: Geography
title: Medieval Mappa Mundi
description: A 60-second lesson on Medieval Mappa Mundi.

hook.text: Medieval European maps put Jerusalem at the center and east at the top.

buildup.text: These 'mappa mundi' were religious statements, not navigation tools.

discovery.text: The Hereford map from 1300 shows monsters, mythical lands, and the Garden of Eden.

twist.text: Sailors ignored these maps entirely, relying on practical coastal charts called portolans.

climax.text: Europe had two mapping traditions: one spiritual, one practical. They rarely overlapped.

punchline.text: Maps of faith and maps of navigation lived in different worlds.

quiz.question: What was at the center of medieval European maps?
